Method–provenance summary
Property: Atom‐condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic attack), f⁻ (nucleophilic attack), f⁰, and the dual descriptor for acrolein; headline number reported here is the largest electrophilic index f⁺.
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` ≡ B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI (density-fitting) in PySCF 2.x.
Geometry provenance: single-point calculations on the user-supplied Cartesian coordinates in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/acrolein.xyz (no re-optimization performed; notation: property//input-xyz).
Environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvent).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (0, 1) for the N state; cation (+1, 2) and anion (–1, 2) states for f⁺/f⁻ construction.
Software/backend: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill calling PySCF.
Convergence & warnings: SCF cycles converged; integrity.status = "ok", integrity.trustworthy = true.  Warnings returned by the engine are reproduced verbatim below.

Result (headline)
